purpose: Daily study recommendation should be LLM-curated from context, not merely a fixed topic-pool rotation.
selection_principles: Reflect already studied topics from config/study-progress.json and sources/fos-study scan.
If a candidate topic is already covered by an existing fos-study document, classify it as review/update-existing rather than a new study topic.
After the user creates or updates a study pack, cool down the same or near-duplicate topic in morning recommendations unless the user asks for interview drill practice on it.
Prefer topics that connect candidate experience to first-round interview answers.
Use pools as candidates and guardrails, not as the final decision-maker.
Avoid treating generic DB tuning as the candidate's active weak area.
Keep enough variety across backend depth, F&B/e-Commerce domain, operations, architecture, AI/backend tooling, and live-coding practice.
Explain why each recommendation is useful now.
interest_axes: JPA/MyBatis query and legacy coexistence as interview context, not as a generic weakness
Redis/cache consistency and operational failure modes
observability, incident response, and non-technical communication
AI agent workflow and backend productivity
avoid: Repeating the same DB/Spring/cache pool simply because it remains in config.
Generating a study-pack automatically before the user selects a topic.
Presenting old CJ OliveYoung-derived material as a fresh study topic when the real value is a short refresh or target-company translation.
Over-indexing on old CJ OliveYoung or Kakao Healthcare target context unless explicitly comparing.
